issues.title: Class-based CSS assertions in StatusRow.test.tsx
issues.description: Tests use `container.querySelector('.bg-success')`, `container.querySelector('.bg-warning')`, and `container.querySelector('.bg-destructive')` to assert the visual state of the status dot. These Tailwind class selectors are fragile — if the class names change (e.g. from semantic refactor), tests break without any functional regression. The `StatusRow` component should expose a stable `data-*` or ARIA attribute to convey status.
issues.fix: Add a `data-status` attribute to the dot element in StatusRow.tsx, e.g. `<span data-status={status} className={...} />`, then assert with `expect(container.querySelector('[data-status="operational"]')).toBeInTheDocument()`. Alternatively add `{...tid('status-dot')}` to the element and use `getByTestId` with a `data-status` assertion.

issues.title: Class-based assertions in InlineImageCarousel.test.tsx (Tailwind classes for star state)
issues.description: Three test cases assert star fill state by checking for Tailwind classes `text-yellow-500`, `fill-current`, and `text-muted-foreground` directly on SVG elements via `toHaveClass`. This couples the tests to Tailwind utility names. A class rename or theme token migration would silently break these assertions without any behavioral change. Additionally, `text-yellow-500` is a raw Tailwind palette color rather than a semantic token.
issues.fix: Replace Tailwind class assertions with a `data-filled` boolean attribute on each star element in the component, e.g. `<Star data-filled={isFilled} />`. Then assert with `toHaveAttribute('data-filled', 'true')`. This also removes the `no-node-access` ESLint suppression comments.

issues.title: Class-based state assertions in RatingStars.test.tsx
issues.description: Four test cases check star fill state by filtering `container.querySelectorAll('svg')` and matching `.includes('fill-current')` and `.includes('text-muted-foreground')` on the class attribute string. These are Tailwind class names used as state proxies. The eslint suppression `/* eslint-disable testing-library/no-node-access */` signals this is an acknowledged workaround, but the pattern remains fragile.
issues.fix: Add `aria-label` on each star or a `data-filled` attribute so tests can use `getAllByRole('img')` + `toHaveAttribute('data-filled', 'true')` count. This provides a stable assertion surface independent of Tailwind class names.

issues.title: Multiple components in admin/users feature lack individual test files
issues.description: The following components have no corresponding `.test.tsx` file: `Pagination.tsx`, `PermissionGroupCard.tsx`, `RoleBadge.tsx`, `TemplateButtons.tsx`, `UserDelegatesCard.tsx`, `UserHeader.tsx`, `UserRow.tsx`, `UserRowWithRole.tsx`, and all five delegates sub-components (`AsDelegateRow.tsx`, `AsSellerRow.tsx`, `PermissionBadges.tsx`, `ProductName.tsx`, `RemoveButton.tsx`). There is only a `UserTable.test.tsx` that exercises the table, but the individual building blocks are untested. For a security-sensitive admin feature (permissions, delegation) this is a meaningful gap.
issues.fix: Create `.test.tsx` files for each untested component, starting with `PermissionGroupCard` and `Pagination` which have the most interactive logic (toggles, page buttons). At minimum cover: renders correctly, handles disabled/pending state (PermissionGroupCard), disables prev/next at boundaries (Pagination), correct badge color per role (RoleBadge).

issues.title: BuyerField, DisplayBlockRenderer, DynamicFormField, and ConfirmActionPanel lack test files
issues.description: Several checkout and received-orders components have no test files: `BuyerField.tsx`, `DisplayBlockRenderer.tsx`, `DynamicFormField.tsx` (checkout) and `ConfirmActionPanel.tsx` (received-orders). These are part of the checkout flow which is security-critical per `checkout-stock-integrity.md`. The stock-integrity rule requires coverage for checkout components.
issues.fix: Add test files for `BuyerField`, `DynamicFormField`, and `ConfirmActionPanel`. For `BuyerField` and `DynamicFormField`, verify they render correct input types and labels. For `ConfirmActionPanel`, verify confirmation/cancel handlers fire correctly.

issues.title: Landing page individual section components only covered by a single shared test file
issues.description: `HeroSection.tsx`, `CtaSection.tsx`, `FeaturesSection.tsx`, and `RolesSection.tsx` are all tested in a single `HomeSections.test.tsx` file, which is an acceptable grouping since these sections have minimal logic. However, `LandingPage.tsx` itself has no test, and the coverage only verifies 3 of the 4 sections (HeroSection is not tested). The HeroSection component is missing from the test file entirely.
issues.fix: Add a test case for `HeroSection` in `HomeSections.test.tsx`. Alternatively, consider adding a test for `LandingPage` that verifies all sections are present when rendered.

patterns.pattern: Strong use of tid() utility across test files
patterns.description: The codebase consistently uses the `tid()` utility to add `data-testid` attributes to components, and tests predominantly use `getByTestId` as the primary selector. This is excellent and aligns with the E2E selectors standard. Over 612 `getByTestId` usages were found across 103 test files.
All test files properly import the Vitest functions they use (`describe`, `it`, `expect`, `vi`, `beforeEach`, `afterEach`) from 'vitest'. No implicit globals were detected.

patterns.pattern: E2E tests avoid hardcoded translation text
patterns.description: A scan of all .spec.ts files found zero uses of `toContainText()` or `toHaveText()` with specific translation strings. E2E tests are locale-independent.

patterns.pattern: Good async handling across test files
patterns.description: Hook tests use `renderHook` + `waitFor`/`findBy*` patterns appropriately for async operations. 18 test files were found using `waitFor` or `findBy` for async assertions.

patterns.pattern: Class-based state assertions remain in a few files
patterns.description: Three test files use CSS class names (Tailwind utilities) as state indicators rather than ARIA attributes or data attributes. All three include eslint-disable-no-node-access comments acknowledging the workaround. These should be refactored to use proper data attributes.
